# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kong API Gateway configuration using declarative YAML. Define services and routes for microservices endpoints. Set up upstream targets and load balancing. This is for our API management platform.
# Category: kong_no_auth

apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1beta1  # version of the configuration, here we use Kubernetes' new feature (networking) which uses an old style YAML format and then a newer one for API specification like Kong does not support yet as per official documentation or any sources
kind: KongIngressConfig   # IngressConfiguration is kind in k8s.io/v1beta1, use this instead of the custom resource we defined above that was introduced only starting from v20 (as Kubernetes' new features) to match it with standard YAML and JSON formatting
metadata: 
  name: my-kong   # Define a unique identifier for your IngressConfiguration instance, e.g., 'my_ingress'. Here also use the old style syntax in kind of v1beta X where we don't need to specify version explicitly if already default (as per API documentation and kubectl commands)
  namespace: my-namespace   # Define a unique identifier for your Kubernetes Namespace, e.g., 'my_project'. This is optional when you create the resource in one cluster with multiple namespaces it will use first available if not provided at creation time or default otherwise (depends on configuration)
spec: 
#Services and routes setup - Note that service definition requires an upstream to be set, but no actual routing details as Kong API Gateway does provide route settings through HTTP paths for request method handling. See below example only defining services without any requests mapping defined in the kong/default file (https://github.com/Kong/kubernetes-ingress)
  http:   # Services is an array, each element represents a service and it has to contain servers as well ports which are list of HTTP endpoints on what server you expose your API gateway at this moment for testing purposes please use the actual upstream URLs after routing set. Here we define only routes due Kong's implementation does not support services or middleware yet (Kong documentation suggests using plugins that have been developed, but currently in a very early stage and cannot be used directly).
    - name: my-microservice   # Define the unique identifier for your service as you call it. It is important to use descriptive names which are human readable 
      hosts:                # An array of host/IP combination where API Gateway should route its traffic (mandatory)
        - "127.0.0.1"  
          port: { number }    # Define the Port at HTTP servers on what server you expose your APIGATEWAY for testing purposes, make sure to use a valid numeric PORT value and IP here after routing set as per Kong's documentation (https://github.com/Kong/kubernetes-ingress)
        - "127.0.0.2"    # Add more hosts if needed for redundancy in case of any failure or maintenance on the service respectively 
      paths:                  # An array contains request path to route rules (mandatory, note that Kong does not support Service/Routes yet as you may be able by using plugins) and actual routing through HTTP methods. Here we only have a single value ie '/' but it can include multiple like ['/*', 'api']
        - "/"   # Define the path where to route requests (Note that Kong does not support services or middleware yet as per documentation). 
      protocols:                # An array of HTTP protocol methods which API Gateway supports. Here we are only supporting GET, POST for simplicity but it can be configured by using 'any' if any http method is needed else use your own supported list like ['http', 'https'] (if you need more support add them to the end)
        - "HTTP"   # Define HTTP protocol here as per Kong API Gateway implementation and we only have a single value.  If this field isn’t set, then requests will be routed based on headers if they do not match one of our defined paths or by default ('/') http request path unless otherwise specified in routes rules
